title: Odontosoria chusana
taxonomy: Odontosoria chusana is classified under the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, and family Lindsaeaceae. It is a species of fern in the genus Odontosoria. The Lindsaeaceae family is a group of small to medium-sized ferns, typically found in tropical and subtropical regions. Odontosoria chusana is native to parts of Asia, including China, India, and Southeast Asia. The classification of Odontosoria chusana reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other ferns.
uses: Odontosoria chusana has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in East Asian cultures, where it is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
